days.title: Arrival in Lisbon
days.subtitle: Exploring the charm of the capital

timeline.title: Check-in at Lisbon Dream Hostel
timeline.detail: Arrive at Lisbon Dream Hostel for check-in. The hostel offers a cozy atmosphere, perfect for solo travelers looking to meet others. Make yourself comfortable in your room and get ready for the day ahead.

timeline.title: Explore Alfama District
timeline.detail: Venture into the Alfama district, one of Lisbon's oldest neighborhoods. Wander through its narrow streets, alleys, and colorful buildings, soaking in the rich history that surrounds you. Don't forget to stop at local shops and markets.

timeline.title: 📸 Photospot: Miradouro de Santa Luzia
timeline.detail: This viewpoint offers stunning views over the Alfama district and the Tagus River, making it a perfect backdrop for your photos. Capture the terracotta rooftops and the vibrant colors of the city.
timeline.mapQuery: Miradouro de Santa Luzia, Lisbon, Portugal
timeline.tip: Best angle: stand at the edge of the terrace. Shoot toward the river with the rooftops in the foreground. Best light: morning for softer tones.

timeline.title: Lunch at Time Out Market
timeline.detail: Head to the iconic Time Out Market for lunch, where you can find a variety of local dishes from renowned chefs. Indulge in tapas, seafood, and other delicacies, surrounded by a lively atmosphere.

timeline.title: Fado Music Experience
timeline.detail: Attend a Fado music performance, an essential part of Portuguese culture. Listen to soulful melodies and heartfelt lyrics as you learn about the history of this music genre and its significance in Lisbon.

timeline.title: Taste Your First Pastel de Nata
timeline.detail: No visit to Lisbon is complete without trying a Pastel de Nata. Visit one of the famous bakeries, such as Pastéis de Belém, and savor the creamy custard tart, known for its crisp pastry and sweet flavor.

timeline.title: Visit the Lisbon Cathedral
timeline.detail: Explore the Lisbon Cathedral, an impressive medieval structure that showcases Gothic architecture. Wander through its ancient corridors and admire the beautiful stained glass windows while learning about its historical significance.

timeline.title: Dinner at a Local Restaurant
timeline.detail: Enjoy dinner at a local restaurant specializing in traditional Portuguese cuisine. Relish dishes like bacalhau à brás or polvo à lagareiro, complemented by Portuguese wines, for an authentic culinary experience.

timeline.title: Get Comfortable for the Night
timeline.detail: Return to Lisbon Dream Hostel and unwind after an exciting day. Take some time to relax, perhaps chat with fellow travelers, or plan your next day's adventures in this welcoming environment.

localTips: Purchase a 24-hour public transport ticket for unlimited rides on trams, buses and metro.
Book a table for Fado restaurants, especially during weekends.
Use Google Maps to navigate the steep streets of Alfama.
Try to visit the Miradouros (viewpoints) at sunrise or sunset for fantastic views.
Be cautious of pickpockets in crowded tourist areas.

days.title: Discovering Belem
days.subtitle: A journey through history and sweet treats

timeline.title: Breakfast at Lisbon Dream Hostel
timeline.detail: Start your day with a complimentary breakfast at Lisbon Dream Hostel. The breakfast includes pastries, fresh fruit, yogurt, and coffee to energize you for a day of exploration.

timeline.title: Transport to Belém
timeline.detail: Take Tram 15 or the train from Cais do Sodré to Belém. The scenic ride will take approximately 20 minutes, providing beautiful views along the waterfront.

timeline.title: Activity: Belem Tower
timeline.detail: Visit the historic Belem Tower, a UNESCO World Heritage site, which was built in the early 16th century. Explore its architecture and enjoy the surrounding gardens and river views.

timeline.title: Activity: Jerónimos Monastery
timeline.detail: Explore Jerónimos Monastery, an extraordinary example of Manueline architecture. It houses the tomb of Vasco da Gama and offers intricate carvings and historical exhibits for visitors.

timeline.title: Lunch at Pão Pão, Queijo Queijo
timeline.detail: Enjoy a delicious lunch at Pão Pão, Queijo Queijo, renowned for its fresh sandwiches and local flavors. Don't miss their specialties made with high-quality Portuguese ingredients.
timeline.mapQuery: Pão Pão, Queijo Queijo, Lisbon, Portugal

timeline.title: Food: Taste Pastéis de Belém
timeline.detail: Visit the famous Pastéis de Belém bakery and indulge in their iconic custard tarts. Made from a secret recipe, these treats are a must-try while in Lisbon.

timeline.title: 📸 Photospot: MAAT
timeline.detail: Capture stunning architectural contrasts at the MAAT (Museum of Art, Architecture and Technology). The modern design against the river creates striking visuals.
timeline.tip: Best angle: stand on the wooden walkway. Shoot toward the building at sunset for golden light reflections. Include the river in your shot.

timeline.title: Stroll along the Tagus River
timeline.detail: Take a leisurely stroll along the Tagus River banks. Enjoy the sights, people-watching, and beautiful landscapes as you reflect on your day in Belém.

timeline.title: Dinner at Restaurante A Margem
timeline.detail: Dine at Restaurante A Margem for delicious Portuguese cuisine. With its spectacular riverfront view, you'll enjoy the ambiance while trying traditional dishes made with local ingredients.

timeline.title: Return to Lisbon Dream Hostel
timeline.detail: Head back to Lisbon Dream Hostel after a full day of exploration. Relax and prepare for tomorrow's adventures while enjoying the hostel's friendly atmosphere.

localTips: Arrive early at Belem to avoid long lines for the Pasteis de Belem.
Buy tickets online for Jerónimos Monastery to skip the queue.
Consider taking tram 15 from downtown Lisbon to Belem.
Check the opening times for different sites as they vary.
Enjoy a walk along the river Tagus for scenic views.

days.title: Trade and Culture in Lisbon
days.subtitle: From Mercado da Ribeira to Chiado

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy a complimentary breakfast at Lisbon Dream Hostel. The spread includes local pastries, fresh fruit, and coffee, perfect for fueling your day ahead.

timeline.title: Visit Mercado da Ribeira
timeline.detail: Explore one of the best food markets in Lisbon, Mercado da Ribeira. Try various local dishes from different vendors and soak in the vibrant atmosphere.

timeline.title: 📸 Photospot: Rua Augusta Arch
timeline.detail: Capture the stunning views from the Rua Augusta Arch, an iconic landmark that offers a panoramic view of the city and river. It's perfect for cityscape photography.
timeline.tip: Best angle: stand in front of the arch. Shoot toward the square. Best light: morning or late afternoon for softer shadows.

timeline.title: Lunch at Time Out Market
timeline.detail: Enjoy a delicious lunch in Time Out Market where various chefs and restaurants offer their unique dishes. Explore Portuguese classics like Bacalhau à Brás and pastel de nata.

timeline.title: Explore Chiado District
timeline.detail: Spend the afternoon wandering around the Chiado district, known for its charming streets filled with shops, cafes, and remarkable historic sites. Don't miss the famous A Brasileira café.

timeline.title: Coffee Break at A Brasileira
timeline.detail: Take a break at the historic A Brasileira café. It's one of the oldest in the city, renowned for its interesting decor and significance in Portuguese history. Try their coffee with a pastel de nata.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel
timeline.detail: Head back to Lisbon Dream Hostel to relax. Take this time to recharge before heading out for dinner and nightlife in Bairro Alto.

timeline.title: Dinner in Bairro Alto
timeline.detail: Indulge in a delightful dinner at one of Bairro Alto's trendy restaurants. Experience local cuisine with tapas and a glass of Vinho Verde while enjoying the lively atmosphere.

timeline.title: Explore Bairro Alto Nightlife
timeline.detail: After dinner, experience the vibrant nightlife of Bairro Alto. Discover local bars with live music, or simply enjoy the atmosphere as locals and travelers mingle.

localTips: Get to Mercado da Ribeira early for the best food selection.
Explore the underground of Chiado for historical spots.
Try to find a rooftop bar for evening drinks.
Visit the free art galleries in Bairro Alto.
Don't miss the chance to try a Bifana sandwich.

days.title: Exploring Sintra
days.subtitle: A day trip to fairy-tale landscapes

timeline.title: Breakfast at the Hotel
timeline.detail: Start your day with a delightful breakfast at Lisbon Dream Hostel. Enjoy fresh pastries, fruits, coffee, and traditional Portuguese items to fuel your adventure in Sintra.

timeline.title: Transport to Sintra
timeline.detail: Travel from Lisbon to Sintra via the scenic train ride. The journey takes approximately 40 minutes. Tickets can be easily purchased at Rossio Station, giving you beautiful views along the way.

timeline.title: Visit Pena Palace
timeline.detail: Explore the vibrant and whimsical architecture of Pena Palace, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Walk through its colourful courtyards, impressive towers, and enjoy breathtaking views of Sintra's lush landscapes.

timeline.title: Lunch at Tascantiga
timeline.detail: Savor a delectable range of tapas at Tascantiga, known for its creative Portuguese dishes. The cozy atmosphere and local ingredients make it a must-visit for food lovers exploring Sintra.

timeline.title: Visit Quinta da Regaleira
timeline.detail: Uncover the enchanting secrets of Quinta da Regaleira. Wander through its mystical gardens, impressive architecture, and ethereal wells that seem to lead to another world, rich in symbolism and history.

timeline.title: 📸 Photospot: Pena Palace Viewpoint
timeline.detail: Capture stunning panoramic photos of Pena Palace from the viewpoint at the north side of the estate. The vibrant colors and intricate details of the palace against the backdrop of lush greenery are breathtaking.
timeline.tip: Best angle: stand on the stone wall facing the palace. Shoot toward the palace with the forest in the foreground. Best light: golden hour.

timeline.title: Transport back to Lisbon
timeline.detail: Return to Lisbon via the train journey from Sintra to Rossio Station. This is a great time to relax and enjoy the scenic views of the Portuguese countryside on your way back.

timeline.title: Dinner at Time Out Market
timeline.detail: End your day with a delicious dinner at Time Out Market, where you can sample a variety of local dishes from renowned chefs. Enjoy the vibrant atmosphere and the diversity of Portuguese cuisine.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel
timeline.detail: After dinner, head back to Lisbon Dream Hostel to relax after an exciting day. Reflect on your adventures in Sintra and enjoy the local ambiance before winding down for the night.

localTips: Take the train from Rossio Station in Lisbon to Sintra; buy round-trip tickets.
Consider a guided tour to cover more attractions in one day.
Visit the Palacio de Pena early to beat the crowds.
Wear comfortable shoes for the hilly terrain.
Try local pastries like Travesseiros while in Sintra.

hotels.name: Lisbon Dream Hostel
hotels.note: Located near Marques de Pombal, walkable to major attractions

urgent.label: Sintra train tickets
urgent.note: Train tickets should be purchased on arrival
